Title: Regulation of Oncogene Expression in T-DNA-Transformed Host Plant Cells

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses ARF5-dependent activation of the Ipt promoter, thereby reducing Ipt expression and cytokinin accumulation potential.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IAA12 significantly reduced ARF5-driven Ipt promoter luciferase activity in protoplasts. 2. Auxin relieves this repression, consistent with canonical auxin signaling. 3. Reduced IAA12 expression in wrky mutant crown galls was proposed to partially compensate for lowered WRKY activity by permitting more ARF5 function.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protoplast transactivation assay with ARF5 + IAA12; promoter-luciferase assay; qRT-PCR
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Reduced in wrky single mutant crown galls relative to wild type (reported in S6C Fig.)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low-level context-dependent expression in crown gall tissues; acts in auxin signaling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a primary focal gene for direct nutrient metabolism but functionally essential in the regulatory module controlling Ipt oncogene expression. In Fig. 8/model, auxin promotes TIR1-SKP(ASK1)-CUL1-mediated polyubiquitination and degradation of IAA12.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating IAA12 could tune auxin-responsive Ipt expression, but likely causes strong pleiotropic developmental effects due to central auxin signaling role.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes cell division and crown gall proliferation/development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher Ipt expression promotes cytokinin accumulation and crown gall development; its transcription is the major oncogene output strongly activated in plant cells.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ipt transcript levels were much higher than IaaH and IaaM in Arabidopsis crown galls. 2. Its promoter was specifically activated by host WRKY18, WRKY40, WRKY60 and ARF5, with WRKY40 strongest. 3. ARF5 and auxin enhanced Ipt promoter activity, and AuxRE mutations abolished this response. 4. Ipt transcription was moderately reduced in the wrky18/40/60 triple mutant early after infection. 5. Crown gall tissues had greatly elevated cytokinin levels, consistent with Ipt action.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point at the transcriptional level in transformed plant cells
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly drives biosynthesis of the target metabolite class
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in crown galls; promoter-GFP and promoter-LUC assays; transcription factor screening; AuxRE mutagenesis; wrky mutant infection assay; cytokinin profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in Arabidopsis crown gall tissues; promoter most active among the three oncogene promoters tested
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native IGR2/Ipt promoter; reporter constructs; CaMV35S used for TF effector expression
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray data of Arabidopsis crown galls cited from previous studies [20,46]; accession NA in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper studies transcriptional regulation of the bacterial ipt gene in plant cells rather than enzyme biochemistry directly. Dominant cytokinin forms in crown galls were zeatin conjugates and zeatin riboside, with free zeatin also elevated.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium tumefaciens infection; auxin (1-NAA); trans-zeatin treatment
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promotes tumorigenesis/crown gall formation due to excessive cytokinin production
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful conceptual target to control Agrobacterium-induced tumorigenesis; promoter/regulatory motif engineering could reduce pathogen-driven cytokinin overproduction.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to elevated auxin levels that support crown gall growth and indirectly boost Ipt expression via ARF5
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive low basal expression contributes to auxin production in transformed tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IaaH transcript abundance was much lower than Ipt in crown galls. 2. The intergenic region IGR1a functions as a promoter in plant cells. 3. No transcription factor from the screen activated the IaaH promoter. 4. Auxin and cytokinin treatments did not affect IaaH promoter activity in protoplasts.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; promoter-GFP assay; promoter-LUC assay; 5' RACE
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low expression in crown galls relative to ipt
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Treated as basally expressed in plant cells; no activating host TF identified in this study. Its role is discussed mainly as support for increased auxin pool over time.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium infection; auxin and cytokinin treatments in protoplast assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential pathogen-control target, but this paper did not identify a strong host regulatory handle for this promoter.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supplies auxin precursor for elevated IAA production during crown gall development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive low basal expression contributes to auxin biosynthesis precursor supply in transformed tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IaaM transcript abundance was much lower than Ipt in crown galls. 2. The intergenic region IGR1b functions as a promoter in plant cells. 3. No transcription factor from the screen activated the IaaM promoter. 4. Auxin and cytokinin treatments did not affect IaaM promoter activity in protoplasts.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; promoter-GFP assay; promoter-LUC assay; 5' RACE
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low expression in crown galls relative to ipt
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Like iaaH, considered mainly basally expressed with no identified activating host TF in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium infection; auxin and cytokinin treatments in protoplast assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential pathogen-control target, though no dominant host transcriptional activator was identified here.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes cell division and crown gall growth through enhanced Ipt expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation of the bacterial Ipt promoter via W-box binding; synergistic co-activation with ARF5
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates Ipt; strongest among tested WRKYs; no activation reported for IaaH or IaaM
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms homo-/heterotypic interactions with WRKY18/40/60 and interacts with ARF5; WRKY40-ARF5 complex potentiates Ipt promoter activation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Agrobacterium infection; pathogen defense signaling; wounding
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes Ipt expression and thereby supports cytokinin accumulation and crown gall development.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Dominant WRKY activator of the Ipt oncogene promoter; strongest single TF effect in the study
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRKY40 strongly activated the Ipt promoter in Arabidopsis protoplasts. 2. WRKY40 transcript was induced early after Agrobacterium infection and elevated in crown galls. 3. wrky40 mutants formed smaller crown galls, and wrky18/40 and wrky18/40/60 mutants showed even stronger defects. 4. WRKY40 bound directly to the Ipt promoter in EMSA. 5. WRKY40 interacted with ARF5 in nuclei and synergistically enhanced Ipt promoter activity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protoplast transactivation screen; qRT-PCR; mutant crown gall assay; BiFC; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced as early as 2 hpi; elevated in crown gall tumors relative to GV3101 controls
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Early infection-responsive and high in crown gall tissues
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S for TF expression; native Ipt promoter-LUC reporter
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray data of infected Arabidopsis tissues/crown galls from previous studies [20,46]; accession NA in provided text
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The direct Ipt promoter probe used in EMSA contained three W-boxes. WRKY40 had stronger activating capacity than WRKY18 or WRKY60, even when all three WRKYs were combined.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of WRKY40 reduced crown gall growth, but WRKY40 also functions broadly in pathogen/stress responses, so manipulation may cause immunity-related pleiotropy.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential host susceptibility factor for engineering reduced Agrobacterium tumorigenesis by attenuating WRKY40-Ipt promoter interaction, though broader defense phenotypes must be considered.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhances Ipt expression and thus cytokinin production promoting crown gall growth
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ARF family; C-terminal domains III and IV required for interaction
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Auxin-dependent transcriptional activation of Ipt via AuxRE binding; synergistic co-activation with WRKY40
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates Ipt in an auxin-dependent manner; no activation of IaaH or IaaM reported
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms self-interactions and heteromeric interactions with WRKY18/40/60; WRKY40-ARF5 complex potentiates Ipt activation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely feedforward-like regulation because auxin accumulation promotes ARF5 activity, which induces Ipt and may further alter hormone balance; explicit loop not directly demonstrated
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates the Ipt promoter in an auxin-dependent manner and synergizes with WRKY40 to boost Ipt expression.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent co-activator linking elevated auxin to stronger Ipt expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ARF5 specifically activated the Ipt promoter in protoplasts. 2. Auxin further enhanced ARF5-mediated Ipt promoter activation. 3. Mutation of AuxREs abolished auxin and ARF5 responsiveness of the Ipt promoter. 4. ARF5 interacted with WRKY40 in the nucleus, and this combination gave stronger activation than either alone. 5. IAA12 repressed ARF5-mediated activation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protoplast transactivation assay; auxin treatment; AuxRE mutagenesis; BiFC
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low through 72 hpi in leaf infiltration assay; elevated in crown galls relative to GV3101 stems; increased in wrky mutant crown galls
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in crown gall tissues and responsive to auxin context
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S for TF expression; native and AuxRE-mutated Ipt promoter-LUC reporters
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray data cited from previous studies [20,46]; accession NA in provided text
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: arf5 mutant analysis in crown gall assays was not feasible because of severe developmental phenotypes. ARF5Δ722 lacking C-terminal domains III/IV lost interaction with WRKYs and self-interaction.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong developmental pleiotropy limits direct genetic use, as arf5 mutants have severe developmental phenotypes.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Direct editing of ARF5 is risky due to developmental essentiality; more practical strategies may target specific Ipt AuxREs or pathogen-induced ARF5-Ipt interactions.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports cytokinin-related crown gall development through Ipt promoter activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates Ipt promoter in protoplasts; weaker than WRKY40
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms interactions with itself, WRKY40, WRKY60, and ARF5 in nucleus
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of Ipt promoter activity and crown gall development, but less dominant than WRKY40.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supportive activator rather than the dominant regulator
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRKY18 activated the Ipt promoter in protoplasts. 2. Its transcript was elevated in crown galls. 3. wrky18 mutants formed smaller crown galls. 4. Co-expression with ARF5 did not produce the same synergistic enhancement seen with WRKY40.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protoplast transactivation assay; qRT-PCR; mutant crown gall assay; BiFC
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced later than WRKY40/60 after infiltration; significantly increased from 8 hpi with GV3101 and elevated in crown galls
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Infection-responsive and elevated in crown gall tissues
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S for TF expression; native Ipt promoter-LUC reporter
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray data cited from previous studies [20,46]; accession NA in provided text
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of the WRKY18/40/60 paralogous module; contributes to phenotype especially in higher-order mutants.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible host susceptibility factor, though probably less efficient target than WRKY40 alone.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports cytokinin-related crown gall development through Ipt promoter activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates Ipt promoter in protoplasts; weaker than WRKY40
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms interactions with itself, WRKY18, WRKY40, and ARF5 in nucleus
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of Ipt promoter activity and crown gall development, but less dominant than WRKY40.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supportive activator rather than the dominant regulator
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRKY60 activated the Ipt promoter in protoplasts. 2. It was induced early after Agrobacterium infiltration and elevated in crown galls. 3. wrky60 mutants formed smaller crown galls. 4. Co-expression with ARF5 did not show the strong synergy observed for WRKY40.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protoplast transactivation assay; qRT-PCR; mutant crown gall assay; BiFC
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Significantly induced from 4 hpi after infiltration and elevated in crown galls
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Early infection-responsive and elevated in crown gall tissues
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S for TF expression; native Ipt promoter-LUC reporter
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray data cited from previous studies [20,46]; accession NA in provided text
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contributes with WRKY18/40 in partially redundant fashion; strongest crown gall phenotype observed in combined mutants.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for reducing Agrobacterium susceptibility in combination with other WRKYs.
